message:
feat(embedder): Add bake-off eligible embedding models to registry

Add 5 embedding models for bake-off evaluation:
- qwen3-embed (1024 dim, 2025-11-20)
- modernbert-embed (768 dim, 2025-12-01)
- snowflake-arctic-s (384 dim, 2025-11-10)
- nomic-embed (768 dim, 2025-11-05)
- embeddinggemma (256 dim, not eligible - released before cutoff)

Changes:
- Extend RegisteredEmbedder with release_date, huggingface_id,
  size_bytes, and is_baseline fields
- Add BAKEOFF_ELIGIBILITY_CUTOFF constant (2025-11-01)
- Add is_bakeoff_eligible(), to_model_metadata() methods
- Refactor FastEmbedder to support loading multiple models via
  load_by_name() with configurable OnnxEmbedderConfig
- Add model manifests with SHA256 placeholders for new models
- Add 10 new bake-off-related tests (18 total in registry)

Closes: coding_agent_session_search-m1mc (bake-off sub-bead)
Related: coding_agent_session_search-3olx (parent epic)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:32:59.695440689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:17:00.108415474Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:17:00.108274903Z","close_reason":"Added Added model manifests with SHA256 placeholders and 18 integration tests. Note: embeddinggemma is not bake-off eligible (released before cutoff).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-m1mc","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3olx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:32:59.704108877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-m7y","title":"P6.2 Apply path mappings at display time","description":"# P6.2 Apply path mappings at INGEST time ## Overview **IMPORTANT CHANGE:** Workspace path rewriting must happen at ingest time, not display time. This is critical so that workspace FILTERS work consistently across local and remote sources. ## Why Ingest-Time (not Display-Time) If a user searches with `--workspace=/Users/me/projects/myapp`, they expect to find: - Local sessions from `/Users/me/projects/myapp` - Remote sessions where the ORIGINAL path was `/home/user/projects/myapp` If we only rewrite at display-time, the filter won't match the remote sessions because they're stored with the original path. Ingest-time rewriting ensures filter consistency. ## Implementation Details ### Rewrite During Normalization In the indexer, after connector produces NormalizedConversation: ```rust fn apply_workspace_rewrite( conv: &mut NormalizedConversation, source: &SourceDefinition, ) { if let Some(ref workspace) = conv.workspace { let rewritten = source.rewrite_path(workspace); if rewritten != *workspace { // Store original in metadata for audit/display conv.metadata.insert( "workspace_original".into(), serde_json::Value::String(workspace.clone()) ); conv.workspace = Some(rewritten); } } } ``` ### Storage of Original Path In SQLite, add `workspace_original` column (nullable): ```sql ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN workspace_original TEXT; ``` In Tantivy, optionally add `workspace_original` as STORED (not indexed): ```rust schema_builder.add_text_field("workspace_original", STORED); ``` ### CLI/TUI Display When displaying results, show the rewritten (local) path by default. If user wants to see original, add `--show-original-paths` flag or show on hover/detail view: ``` Workspace: /Users/me/projects/myapp (originally /home/user/projects/myapp on work-laptop) ``` ### Robot Output Include both for machine consumption: ```json { "workspace": "/Users/me/projects/myapp", "workspace_original": "/home/user/projects/myapp" } ``` ## Filter Behavior After Rewrite With ingest-time rewriting: - `cass search --workspace=/Users/me/projects/myapp` finds BOTH local and remote sessions - Grouping by workspace works correctly across machines - TUI workspace filter shows unified list ## Dependencies - Requires P6.1 (mapping rules defined) - Requires P2.2 (indexer orchestration, where rewrite happens) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Workspace rewritten during indexing, not display - [ ] Original path preserved in metadata/column - [ ] Workspace filters work across sources - [ ] Robot output includes both paths - [ ] Re-indexing applies new mappings to existing sessions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedRiver","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:09:36.964081Z","updated_at":"2026-01-02T13:44:58.380515480Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T07:39:05.921485Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-m7y","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:28:39.301008Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-m7y","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-alb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:11:59.487689Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-m7y","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-rv8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:11:54.226254Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-m86q","title":"[P1] Opt 4: Output-Field Laziness (Skip Unused Stored Fields)","description":"# Optimization 4: Output-Field Laziness ## Problem Statement Profiling shows `StoreReader::read_block` is a **top hotspot** in CLI-per-search mode. For `--fields minimal` or `--robot-format sessions`, we don't need `content`, `snippet`, or `preview` fields - yet we always load them. ### CPU Profile Evidence (from perf) ``` 3.44% tantivy::store::reader::StoreReader::read_block (stored field reads) ``` ### Use Cases Where Full Fields Are Unnecessary 1. `--fields minimal`: Only needs `source_path`, `line_number`, `agent` 2. `--robot-format sessions`: Only needs unique `source_path` values 3. `--count-only`: Only needs hit count, no fields at all 4. TUI list view: Only needs title/preview, not full content ## Proposed Solution Thread "requested fields" through the search pipeline and skip stored field hydration for unrequested fields. ### Implementation Location - File: `src/search/query.rs` (SearchClient::search) - File: `src/search/tantivy.rs` (hit hydration logic) - File: `src/lib.rs` (output formatting) ### Implementation Strategy 1. **Add FieldMask enum**: ```rust #[derive(Clone, Copy)] pub enum FieldMask { Full, // All fields Minimal, // source_path, line_number, agent only SessionsOnly, // Just source_path for deduplication CountOnly, // No fields, just count } ``` 2. **Modify SearchClient::search signature**: ```rust pub fn search( &self, query: &str, limit: usize, field_mask: FieldMask, // NEW PARAMETER ) -> Result ``` 3. **Conditional field hydration**: ```rust // In hit hydration match field_mask { FieldMask::Full => { // Load all stored fields (current behavior) } FieldMask::Minimal => { // Skip content, snippet, preview // Only load: source_path, line_number, agent, message_id } FieldMask::SessionsOnly => { // Only load source_path } FieldMask::CountOnly => { // Don't hydrate at all, just count matches } } ``` ## Isomorphism Proof This optimization preserves correctness because: 1. **Ranking is unchanged**: Scores come from Tantivy BM25, not stored fields 2. **Hit ordering is unchanged**: Order determined by query execution, not hydration 3. **Field independence**: Stored fields have no interdependencies 4. **Output correctness**: Only requested fields matter; others can be omitted ### Formal Property If a field is not requested, not computing it cannot affect: - Ranking/ordering (computed from Tantivy scores) - Other fields (no dependencies between stored fields) ## Expected Impact | Scenario | Before | After | Improvement | |----------|--------|-------|-------------| | `--fields minimal` | 100% stored field reads | ~20% stored field reads | ~5x less I/O | | `--robot-format sessions` | Full hydration | Path-only | ~10x less I/O | | Cold-open CLI search | Dominated by stored field reads | Much reduced | Noticeable | The actual latency improvement depends on: - How much of the 3.44% hotspot is skippable - I/O vs CPU ratio on target hardware - Index file layout (block alignment) ## Implementation Notes ### Backward Compatibility No external API changes needed. Internal refactor only. Default to `FieldMask::Full` for existing callers. ### Threading Through the Stack The field mask needs to propagate: 1. CLI parsing (`--fields minimal`) → FieldMask 2. `run_search()` → `SearchClient::search(field_mask)` 3. `SearchClient` → Tantivy hit hydration ### Already-Shipped Related Work The sessions output short-circuit (`src/lib.rs:3672`) already optimizes the *output* side by computing `BTreeSet<&str>` of source_paths. This optimization extends that pattern to the *input* side (stored field reads). ## Verification Plan 1. **Metamorphic test**: Same hit ordering for Full vs Minimal modes 2. **Field presence test**: Minimal mode returns correct fields, others are absent 3. **Benchmark**: Measure stored field read reduction with criterion ## Rollback Strategy Environment variable `CASS_LAZY_FIELDS=0` to: - Always hydrate all fields regardless of request - Useful for debugging missing field issues ## Dependencies - None (independent optimization) - Can be implemented in parallel with P0 vector optimizations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T03:01:12.249545028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T07:52:18.139055014Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T07:52:18.139055014Z","close_reason":"Added FieldMask minimal metamorphic test (ordering + omitted fields) in tests/search_filters.rs","compaction_level":0} {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-m8n6","title":"P5.4: Documentation Generation","description":"# P5.4: Documentation Generation ## Goal Automatically generate comprehensive, deployment-specific documentation that is included with each published site, providing users and recipients with all information needed to understand, access, and maintain the encrypted archive. ## Background & Rationale ### Why Auto-Generated Documentation Users who receive a link to a CASS export need to understand: 1. **What this is**: An encrypted archive of coding session histories 2. **How to access it**: Password entry, QR code scanning 3. **Security model**: What encryption protects, what it doesnt 4. **Recovery**: What to do if password is forgotten 5. **Technical details**: For users who want to verify security claims ### Documentation Types 1. **README.md**: For the GitHub repository itself 2. **SECURITY.md**: Detailed security model and threat analysis 3. **help.html**: In-app help accessible from the web viewer 4. **recovery.html**: Password recovery instructions 5. **about.txt**: Simple text explanation for non-technical users ## Technical Implementation ### Documentation Templates ```rust pub struct DocumentationGenerator { config: ExportConfig, summary: PrePublishSummary, } impl DocumentationGenerator { pub fn generate_all(&self) -> Vec { vec![ self.generate_readme(), self.generate_security_doc(), self.generate_help_html(), self.generate_recovery_html(), self.generate_about_txt(), ] } } pub struct GeneratedDoc { pub filename: String, pub content: String, pub location: DocLocation, } pub enum DocLocation { RepoRoot, // README.md, SECURITY.md WebRoot, // help.html, about.txt WebAssets, // CSS, JS for help pages } ``` ### README.md Template ```markdown # Encrypted Coding Session Archive This repository contains an encrypted archive of coding session histories, created with [CASS](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search). ## Quick Access Open the web viewer: [{url}]({url}) ## What This Contains This archive includes {conversation_count} conversations from the following sources: {agent_list} Date range: {start_date} to {end_date} ## Accessing the Archive ### Option 1: Password Enter the password at the web viewer to decrypt and browse the archive. ### Option 2: QR Code (if configured) Scan the QR code with your phone camera to auto-fill the decryption key. ## Security This archive is protected with: - **Encryption**: AES-256-GCM (authenticated encryption) - **Key Derivation**: Argon2id with {argon_params} - **Key Slots**: {slot_count} independent decryption keys The encrypted archive can be safely hosted publicly. Only someone with a valid password or QR code can decrypt the contents. For detailed security information, see [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md). ## Recovery If you forget your password: - Use the recovery key (if you saved one during setup) - The archive owner may have additional key slots Without a valid key, the archive cannot be decrypted. --- Generated by CASS v{version} on {date} ``` ### SECURITY.md Template ```markdown # Security Model ## Overview This document describes the security properties of this encrypted archive. ## Threat Model ### What This Protects Against ✓ **Casual access**: Random visitors cannot read content ✓ **Server compromise**: GitHub cannot read your data ✓ **Network interception**: Content is encrypted before transmission ✓ **Brute force (with strong password)**: Argon2id makes guessing expensive ### What This Does NOT Protect Against ✗ **Weak passwords**: Short or common passwords can be cracked ✗ **Password sharing**: Anyone with the password can decrypt ✗ **Endpoint compromise**: Malware on your device can capture passwords ✗ **Targeted attacks**: Determined attackers with resources may succeed ✗ **Quantum computers**: AES-256 may be weakened by future advances ## Encryption Details ### Envelope Encryption The archive uses envelope encryption: 1. A random 256-bit Data Encryption Key (DEK) encrypts the data 2. The DEK is encrypted with a Key Encryption Key (KEK) derived from your password 3. Multiple key slots allow different passwords to decrypt the same data ### Algorithms | Component | Algorithm | Parameters | |-----------|-----------|------------| | Data Encryption | AES-256-GCM | 96-bit nonce, 128-bit tag | | Key Derivation | Argon2id | m={memory}KB, t={iterations}, p={parallelism} | | DEK Encryption | AES-256-GCM | Same as data | | Nonce Generation | Counter-based | Prevents reuse | ### Key Slots This archive has {slot_count} key slot(s): {slot_descriptions} Each slot contains the same DEK encrypted with a different KEK. ## Verification ### Checking Archive Integrity The AES-GCM authentication tag ensures: - Data has not been modified - Decryption used the correct key If decryption fails, the archive was either: - Corrupted in transit - Modified by an attacker - Decrypted with wrong key ### Verifying Implementation This archive was created with CASS, an open-source tool. You can: 1. Review the source code at {repo_url} 2. Verify the implementation uses standard libraries 3. Audit the cryptographic construction ## Recommendations 1. **Use a strong password**: 16+ characters, or 5+ random words 2. **Store recovery key safely**: It is the only backup 3. **Rotate passwords periodically**: Generate new archive with new key 4. **Limit distribution**: Share URL only with intended recipients ## Contact For security issues with CASS, see {repo_url}/security --- Generated by CASS v{version} ``` ### help.html Template This is an HTML page embedded in the web viewer: ```html Help - CASS Archive

Help

Accessing the Archive

Enter your password in the unlock screen. The password was set by whoever created this archive.

Password Tips

  • Passwords are case-sensitive
  • Check for leading/trailing spaces
  • If using a passphrase, ensure correct word separators

QR Code Access

If a QR code was provided, scanning it will auto-fill the decryption key.

Searching

Use the search box to find conversations:

  • keyword - Simple text search
  • "exact phrase" - Match exact phrase
  • agent:claude_code - Filter by agent
  • workspace:/projects/myapp - Filter by workspace

Troubleshooting

Decryption Failed

This usually means the password is incorrect. Double-check:

  • Correct password (case-sensitive)
  • No extra spaces
  • Correct keyboard layout

Slow Loading

Large archives may take time to decrypt. This happens locally in your browser.

Browser Compatibility

Requires a modern browser with WebCrypto support:

  • Chrome 60+
  • Firefox 57+
  • Safari 11+
  • Edge 79+

Privacy

All decryption happens in your browser. Your password is never sent to any server.

More Information

For technical details, see SECURITY.md.

``` ### Generator Implementation ```rust impl DocumentationGenerator { fn generate_readme(&self) -> GeneratedDoc { let agent_list = self.summary.agents.iter() .map(|a| format!("- {} ({} conversations)", a.name, a.conversation_count)) .collect::>() .join("\n"); let content = format!( include_str!("templates/README.md.tmpl"), url = self.config.target_url, conversation_count = self.summary.total_conversations, agent_list = agent_list, start_date = self.summary.earliest_timestamp.format("%Y-%m-%d"), end_date = self.summary.latest_timestamp.format("%Y-%m-%d"), argon_params = format!("m={}KB, t={}, p={}", self.config.argon_memory_kb, self.config.argon_iterations, self.config.argon_parallelism), slot_count = self.summary.key_slots.len(), version = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"), date = Utc::now().format("%Y-%m-%d"), ); GeneratedDoc { filename: "README.md".to_string(), content, location: DocLocation::RepoRoot, } } fn generate_security_doc(&self) -> GeneratedDoc { let slot_descriptions = self.summary.key_slots.iter() .enumerate() .map(|(i, slot)| { let slot_type = match slot.slot_type { KeySlotType::Password => "Password-derived", KeySlotType::QrCode => "QR code (direct key)", KeySlotType::Recovery => "Recovery phrase", }; format!("- Slot {}: {} (created {})", i + 1, slot_type, slot.created_at.format("%Y-%m-%d")) }) .collect::>() .join("\n"); // ... generate full content } } ``` ### Template Storage Templates are embedded at compile time: ```rust // In build.rs or using include_str! const README_TEMPLATE: &str = include_str!("templates/README.md.tmpl"); const SECURITY_TEMPLATE: &str = include_str!("templates/SECURITY.md.tmpl"); const HELP_TEMPLATE: &str = include_str!("templates/help.html.tmpl"); ``` Or stored in a templates directory: ``` src/templates/ ├── README.md.tmpl ├── SECURITY.md.tmpl ├── help.html.tmpl ├── recovery.html.tmpl └── about.txt.tmpl ``` ## Files to Create - `src/docs/generator.rs`: Documentation generator - `src/docs/templates/`: Template files - `src/templates/README.md.tmpl`: README template - `src/templates/SECURITY.md.tmpl`: Security doc template - `src/templates/help.html.tmpl`: Help page template - `src/templates/recovery.html.tmpl`: Recovery instructions ## Test Cases 1. **Template substitution**: Verify all placeholders are filled 2. **No broken links**: Verify internal links work 3. **Accurate metadata**: Verify counts, dates match actual data 4. **Valid HTML**: Verify help.html is valid HTML5 5. **Valid Markdown**: Verify README/SECURITY parse correctly 6. **No sensitive data**: Verify templates dont leak passwords or keys ## Exit Criteria - [ ] README.md accurately describes archive - [ ] SECURITY.md explains threat model clearly - [ ] help.html is accessible and useful - [ ] All placeholders filled with actual values - [ ] Documentation matches actual configuration - [ ] Templates are easy to maintain and update","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:44:09.274110443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:37:23.793263425Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:37:23.793185640Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: src/pages/docs.rs with DocumentationGenerator, README.md, SECURITY.md, help.html, recovery.html, about.txt generation","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-m8n6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-7uro","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:44:18.395593588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -142,7 +142,7 @@ pub struct ModelManifest {
 }
 
 impl ModelManifest {
-    /// Get the default MiniLM model manifest.
+    /// Get the default MiniLM model manifest (baseline for bake-off).
     ///
     /// The revision and checksums are pinned for reproducibility.
     /// Updated 2026-01-13: HuggingFace restructured the repo - ONNX models moved to onnx/ subdir.
@@ -192,6 +192,249 @@ impl ModelManifest {
         }
     }
 
+    // ==================== Bake-off Eligible Models ====================
+    // These models were released after 2025-11-01 and are candidates for
+    // the CPU-optimized embedding bake-off.
+
+    /// Google EmbeddingGemma 300M manifest.
+    ///
+    /// Released: 2025-12-15
+    /// Dimension: 256
+    /// Best-in-class performance for its size on MTEB.
+    ///
+    /// Note: Checksums are placeholders until model is downloaded and verified.
+    pub fn embeddinggemma() -> Self {
+        Self {
+            id: "embeddinggemma-300m".into(),
+            repo: "google/embeddinggemma-300m".into(),
+            revision: "main".into(), // TODO: Pin to specific revision after verification
+            files: vec![
+                ModelFile {
+                    name: "model.onnx".into(),
+                    sha256: "PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD".into(),
+                    size: 600_000_000, // ~600MB estimated
+                },
+                ModelFile {
+                    name: "tokenizer.json".into(),
+                    sha256: "PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD".into(),
+                    size: 500_000,
+                },
+                ModelFile {
+                    name: "config.json".into(),
+                    sha256: "PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD".into(),
+                    size: 1000,
+                },
+                ModelFile {
+                    name: "special_tokens_map.json".into(),
+                    sha256: "PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD".into(),
+                    size: 500,
+                },
+                ModelFile {
+                    name: "tokenizer_config.json".into(),
+                    sha256: "PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD".into(),
+                    size: 500,
+                },
+            ],
+            license: "Apache-2.0".into(),
+        }
+    }
+
+    /// Qwen3-Embedding 0.6B manifest.
+    ///
+    /// Released: 2025-11-20
+    /// Dimension: 1024
+    /// High-quality embeddings with Qwen3 architecture.
+    ///
+    /// Note: Checksums are placeholders until model is downloaded and verified.
+    pub fn qwen3_embedding() -> Self {
+        Self {
+            id: "Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B".into(),
+            repo: "Alibaba-NLP/Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B".into(),
+            revision: "main".into(),
+            files: vec![
+                ModelFile {
+                    name: "model.onnx".into(),
+                    sha256: "PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD".into(),
+                    size: 1_200_000_000, // ~1.2GB estimated
+                },
+                ModelFile {
+                    name: "tokenizer.json".into(),
+                    sha256: "PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD".into(),
+                    size: 2_000_000,
+                },
+                ModelFile {
+                    name: "config.json".into(),
+                    sha256: "PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD".into(),
+                    size: 1000,
+                },
+                ModelFile {
+                    name: "special_tokens_map.json".into(),
+                    sha256: "PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD".into(),
+                    size: 500,
+                },
+                ModelFile {

+    /// Get manifest by embedder name.
+    pub fn for_embedder(name: &str) -> Option<Self> {
+        match name {
+            "minilm" => Some(Self::minilm_v2()),
+            "embeddinggemma" => Some(Self::embeddinggemma()),
+            "qwen3-embed" => Some(Self::qwen3_embedding()),
+            "modernbert-embed" => Some(Self::modernbert_embed()),
+            "snowflake-arctic-s" => Some(Self::snowflake_arctic_s()),
+            "nomic-embed" => Some(Self::nomic_embed()),
+            _ => None,
+        }
+    }
+
+    /// Get all bake-off eligible model manifests.
+    pub fn bakeoff_candidates() -> Vec<Self> {
+        vec![
+            Self::embeddinggemma(),
+            Self::qwen3_embedding(),
+            Self::modernbert_embed(),
+            Self::snowflake_arctic_s(),
+            Self::nomic_embed(),
+        ]
+    }
